_pending: Wines LANDED 2026-07-09 (Adrian): white = Valle dell'Acate Thymbra Vermentino; red = Murgo Etna Rosso 2023, poured with a slight chill. Cocktail + mocktail LANDED (Katie): Verdant Fields Forever (Terramar gin) + Stone Fruit & Honeysuckle Lemonade. Beef = Swan House primal roast (rib eye/porterhouse/t-bone); guanciale coming down to SF.

header.title: Swan House Farm
header.subtitle: High Summer
header.tagline: thank you so much for joining us on this humble journey through everything skagit and swan house has to offer

courses.label: Antipasti
courses.subtitle: passed welcome bites at sit-down
courses.plateware: Two bites passed together at sit-down. Per setting: 8" bread plate and fork only. Cutlery is staggered course-by-course, not all preset at once.

dishes.name: Stuffed Squash Blossom
dishes.components: goat cheese & guanciale, balsamic, garden herbs
dishes.story: A squash blossom from Kelly's garden, stuffed with a goat cheese and guanciale filling, blistered over live fire on the Big Green Egg, and finished with aged balsamic and garden herbs (thyme leads). Warm, cured, and deep — the savory counterpart to the bright pepper toast it's passed alongside. This started life as a stuffed fig; the blossom is the farm-grown answer using the same filling.

ingredients.note: the edible golden flower of the summer squash plant; delicate and faintly sweet, picked the morning of service
Italian cured pork jowl, richer and silkier than pancetta; house-cured by Tim at the farm
fresh, tangy chevre that anchors the filling and balances the cured pork
a kamado-style ceramic smoker/grill we use for live-fire cooking at the farm

dishes.name: Sweet Pepper Toast
dishes.components: whipped ricotta, marinated pepper, mint, preserved lemon, honey
dishes.story: Grilled Breadfarm focaccia under whipped ricotta, marinated farm sweet peppers, torn mint, a little preserved lemon, and a drizzle of lavender-infused honey. Cool, bright, and sweet-tart, set deliberately against the warm squash blossom so the two bites play off each other.

ingredients.note: lemons cured in salt for several weeks until the rind turns silky and intensely citrusy; we cure ours at San Fermo
fresh Italian whey cheese, soft and milky, whipped light to spread on the toast
honey infused with lavender from the farm's flower wall; floral and gently sweet
Kelly's sweet peppers, marinated for brightness and a little acid

courses.label: Insalata
courses.subtitle: Course 1
courses.plateware: Wide shallow bowl or salad plate. Small fork. Butter knife.

dishes.name: Summer Stone Fruit & Stracciatella
dishes.components: grilled and fresh stone fruit, garden greens, stracciatella, tomato-leaf oil
dishes.story: High-summer stone fruit from Snow Goose Produce just up the road — peach and nectarine leading, with a little plum — some grilled for a little smoke and caramel, some left fresh and bright, over a pillow of stracciatella with Kelly's garden greens and a finish of tomato-leaf oil. Juicy and lifted, it sets up the richer pasta behind it.

ingredients.note: peach and nectarine leading, with a little plum, from Snow Goose Produce nearby; a mix of grilled and fresh across the plate
Kelly's lettuces, whatever variety is at its peak this week; tender and delicate, looking beautiful right now
the soft, creamy interior of burrata: torn fresh mozzarella curds folded with cream
oil infused with farm tomato leaves; green and unmistakably of the vine, the one 'tomato' the farm gives us in July

dishes.name: Fazzoletti
dishes.components: cultured brown butter, cured egg yolk, focaccia crumb, parmesan
dishes.story: Fazzoletti means 'little handkerchiefs' in Italian: thin squares of fresh pasta with farm herbs and flowers laminated between two sheets so the petals show through. Joe builds and laminates the sheets at San Fermo; they're cut day-of here at the farm. Dressed in cultured brown butter, grated cured egg yolk, a focaccia pangrattato off the same Breadfarm bread as the toast, parmesan, and lemon. The form is Ligurian, traditionally served with light sauces that show off the herbs inside.

ingredients.note: Italian for 'little handkerchiefs'; thin squares of pasta with herbs and edible flowers laminated between two sheets so the leaves show through. A Ligurian form, classically served with pesto or light sauces
farm egg yolks cured in salt and sugar, then dried and grated like bottarga (boh-TAR-gah, the Italian cured fish roe); deep and savory, it makes the farm's own eggs the luxury of the plate
butter churned from cultured cream, then browned; nuttier than regular butter with a slight tang
toasted, seasoned breadcrumbs, sometimes called 'poor man's parmesan'; here off the Breadfarm focaccia, for crunch
short for Parmigiano-Reggiano, the aged hard Italian cheese from the Parma region

courses.label: Secondi
courses.subtitle: Course 3
courses.plateware: Family-style. Carving board and platters to the table; warm entree plates per guest. Large fork, steak knife.

dishes.name: Swan House Farm Primal Roast
dishes.components: rib eye, porterhouse & t-bone, green garlic crust, charred apricot agrodolce, Walla Walla onions, grilled kale
dishes.story: Swan House Farm's primal roast: the prime middle of Tim's steer, the rib and short loin, rubbed in a green garlic and herb crust and grilled over apple wood on the Big Green Egg, rested, then carved into the rib eye, porterhouse, and t-bone and served family-style. A charred apricot agrodolce is passed alongside, sweet-tart against the apple-wood char, with coal-roasted Walla Walla onions and grilled kale finished with lemon and chili. This is the night's centerpiece, the move off June's plated belly to a shared carve.

ingredients.note: instead of a steak per plate, we roast the whole rib-and-short-loin primal and carve it to share; it yields the rib eye (from the rib) and the porterhouse and t-bone (from the short loin, the porterhouse carrying more tenderloin)
we grill the roast over apple wood on the Big Green Egg; the fruitwood smoke is milder and sweeter than hardwoods like oak or hickory
young garlic pulled before the bulb fully forms; mild and grassy, less sharp than mature garlic. It is the crust on the roast
Italian sweet-and-sour sauce; here charred apricot cooked down sweet-tart to cut the richness of the beef
sweet onions from Walla Walla, Washington; mild enough to roast whole in the coals until jammy

dishes.name: Lemon Verbena Panna Cotta
dishes.story: A silky set cream steeped with lemon verbena from Kelly's garden, with macerated Rainier cherries and their own syrup spooned over, and a scatter of farm flowers. No bake this series, the dessert leans on the cherries' own brightness.

ingredients.note: Italian for 'cooked cream'; a lightly set custard thickened with a little gelatin rather than eggs, so it stays silky and wobbly
a lemon-scented herb from Kelly's garden, brighter and more perfumed than lemon balm; it steeps into the cream
Rainier cherries tossed with a little sugar and left to release their juices into a loose syrup

dishes.name: Cafe Umbria
dishes.story: Locally roasted coffee from Seattle's Cafe Umbria, served to close the meal.

wines.name: Valle dell'Acate Thymbra Vermentino
wines.pour_notes: The Vermentino we already pour by the glass at the restaurant, brought up to the farm. Organic fruit off poor, chalky 'Terra Bianca' soil in southeastern Sicily. Bright straw flecked green and gold; peach, citrus, and a saline snap with a whisper of vanilla on the finish. High-acid and textural, built to carry the herbs, the stracciatella, and the cultured-butter-and-cured-yolk pasta without getting buried.
wines.producer: Valle dell'Acate is a historic family estate in the Acate valley of southeastern Sicily (province of Ragusa), in the Cerasuolo di Vittoria heartland. Thymbra comes from Contrada Biddine Soprana, a small upland plot at about 250 meters on 'Terra Bianca' — very poor but deep chalky-limestone soil. The vines struggle there and stay low in vigor, which concentrates the fruit, while steady ventilation keeps it from over-ripening.
wines.winemaking: Organically farmed Vermentino, spur-cordon trained at 5,000 vines per hectare, hand-harvested in the last days of August. Fermented and aged in stainless steel about four months, then three months in bottle — no oak, so the fruit, salinity, and acid stay front and center. Serve cool, 10-12°C.

wines.name: Murgo Etna Rosso
wines.pour_notes: Adrian's pick for its smoke affinity, poured with a slight chill for July. Nerello Mascalese off the eastern slope of Etna — ruby and lifted, red fruit with a spice edge of black pepper and clove, a mineral, saline core, and fine elegant tannins. Medium-bodied and high-acid, it plays off the apple-wood beef and the sweet-tart agrodolce without weighing the table down.
wines.producer: Cantine Murgo has farmed the eastern flank of Mount Etna since 1860, based at Tenuta San Michele in Santa Venerina. The vineyards sit on volcanic soil where the microclimate is shaped by the nearby Ionian Sea, which lends the reds finesse and an elegant complexity. Murgo has bottled this Etna Rosso since the 1980 harvest.
wines.winemaking: Nerello Mascalese with a little Nerello Cappuccio, espalier-trained at 4,500-5,000 plants per hectare on Etna's volcanic slopes, around 13% alcohol. A restrained, traditional vinification lets the site speak: ruby, sapid, and mineral, built around bright acidity and fine tannins rather than weight or oak. Best with a light chill in warm weather.

cocktails.name: Verdant Fields Forever
cocktails.pour_notes: Katie's summer cooler: Terramar gin and a Bianco vermouth infused with Swan House dill, lengthened with fresh-pressed cucumber, lime, and soda. Bright, herbal, and garden-green, finished with a nasturtium flower and a frond of dill.

ingredients.name: Terramar gin
ingredients.description: Gin from Terramar Brewstillery in Edison, just down the road — an award-winning brewery and distillery set in a former slaughterhouse on the old Edison Slough oxbow. They distill it grain-to-glass from Washington-grown, craft-malted barley (the same base as their beer) and are growing their own juniper and botanicals on the property. A true neighbor pour to open the cocktail.

ingredients.name: Dill-infused Bianco vermouth
ingredients.description: Bianco (sweet white) vermouth infused with dill grown at Swan House. Floral and lightly herbal, the dill ties the glass to the garden.

ingredients.name: Fresh-pressed cucumber
ingredients.description: Pressed to order for a clean, cooling green note.

ingredients.name: Lime
ingredients.description: Fresh lime for brightness and acid.

ingredients.name: Simple syrup
ingredients.description: A touch of sweetness to balance.

ingredients.name: Soda
ingredients.description: Topped with soda for length and lift, then a nasturtium flower and dill to finish.

cocktails.name: Stone Fruit & Honeysuckle Lemonade
cocktails.pour_notes: A no-proof cooler: ripe nectarine and apricot pressed into a lemonade sweetened with honeysuckle syrup from the Swan House garden. Bright, floral, and just tart enough — a real drink for anyone pouring zero-proof.

ingredients.name: Nectarine
ingredients.description: Ripe summer nectarine, pressed into the lemonade.

ingredients.name: Apricot
ingredients.description: Local apricot for stone-fruit depth and color.

ingredients.name: Swan House honeysuckle
ingredients.description: Honeysuckle blossoms from the garden steeped into a floral syrup that sweetens the drink.

ingredients.name: Lemonade
ingredients.description: Fresh lemon and water, the tart backbone.
